summary: Lightpanda installed on both laptop (~/.local/bin/lightpanda) and the cto-v1 VPS (/usr/local/bin/lightpanda) as a CDP-compatible Playwright backend. Drop-in replacement for headless Chromium via connectOverCDP. 11x faster execution and 9x lower memory than headless Chrome on scraping workloads. Ships an MCP server (`lightpanda mcp`) that exposes navigate/click/type/query to LLMs directly, plus a one-shot page-fetch mode for clean JS-rendered HTML.
rationale: John's directive 2026-05-20: 'let's go with it. update the documentation, install it on this computer. So that you can use it and install it on the virtual private server.'
Architectural fit: CDP-compatible, so any existing Playwright script switches with one line (chromium.connectOverCDP('ws://127.0.0.1:9222')). No code rewrite to adopt.
Capability uplift: native MCP server + structured page-fetch mode are net-new tools Chromium doesn't expose. Both hemispheres can consume them via MCP.
Performance: 11x faster, 9x less memory — relevant for the autonomous browser-automation workloads (DNS management, 2FA reading, scraping) that the hemispheres run unattended.

license_exception.rule_violated: AGENTS.md community-signal criteria — 'MIT/Apache-2/BSD/ISC only for direct adoption; GPL/AGPL → fork-trigger BACKLOG entry, do not import.' Rule added 2026-05-20 commit 11c03a3.
exception_basis: Running as-is without source modification — AGPL's copyleft trigger requires modification, not mere use.
CTO repo is already public on GitHub (github.com/johnjhusband/CTO) under MIT — source disclosure is not a new burden imposed by AGPL.
We do not redistribute Lightpanda binaries; we install from upstream releases.
If we ever fork Lightpanda to patch a bug, that fork must be published — accepted as the cost of the exception.
license_exception.decision: Override the rule for this specific adoption. The rule remains correct as a default; this is a documented exception, not a precedent for blanket GPL/AGPL adoption.

maturity_caveat: Beta. 'Stability and coverage improving, many websites work, you may still encounter errors or crashes.' For mission-critical flows (Namecheap stateful login with email-based 2FA, OAuth consent flows), keep Chromium as the default; adopt Lightpanda incrementally for less-fragile workloads first.

community_signals_observed.release_status: nightly only — no stable tag yet
community_signals_observed.note: Standard signals (stars, contributors, weekly downloads) not yet captured. Add when re-evaluating the AGPL exception.

integration_state_2026-05-20.install-cto.sh: updated — future provisions install Lightpanda before the Gmail MCP step
integration_state_2026-05-20.namecheap_playwright: untouched — stays on Chromium for the working stateful login flow. Lightpanda available as an alternate backend via connectOverCDP when scripts opt in.
integration_state_2026-05-20.hemisphere_mcp_configs: not yet wired — Lightpanda MCP is available on disk but no MCP entry added to OpenClaw/Hermes configs; defer until first concrete browser-automation use case in a hemisphere flow

rollback.instructions: On any host: `sudo rm /usr/local/bin/lightpanda` (or `rm ~/.local/bin/lightpanda` on laptop). Remove the install block from scripts/install-cto.sh. No data dependencies to clean up — Lightpanda holds no persistent state.
